steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $7\left(6x^2+x+1\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Find the choice that equals $42x^{2}+7x+7$ by factoring. The GCF of 42, 7, and 7 is 7, giving $7(6x^{2}+x+1)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $6x^2(7x+7)$: expands to $42x^{3}+42x^{2}$, not $42x^{2}+7x+7$. ✗
  • Choice B — $42x(x+1)$: expands to $42x^{2}+42x$, not $42x^{2}+7x+7$. ✗
  • Choice C — $7\left(6x^2+x+1\right)$: expands to $42x^{2}+7x+7$. ✓
  • Choice D — $5\left(3x^2+x+1\right)$: expands to $15x^{2}+5x+5$, not $42x^{2}+7x+7$. ✗
That is the correct answer.
